She was also able to use a [[Gravimetric Spike]] to accurately determine when a portal will open and control the portal. {{Quote|I just want to say that was very, very impressive what you did back there.|[[Thor]] to Mighty Thor|Thor: Love and Thunder}} *'''Master Combatant''': [[File:Mighty Thor vs. Olympians.png|thumb|250px|Mighty Thor fighting against [[Olympians]]]] Foster became a very skilled combatant as a result of [[Mjølnir]]. She was able to become a formidable opponent to [[Gorr the God Butcher]], who was able to kill many other gods. *'''Hammer Mastery''': Mighty Thor was very good at battling with [[Mjølnir]], using it very well despite only having it for a short time. *'''Expert Acrobat''': With [[Mjølnir]], Mighty Thor was able to do many flips and jumps to attack her enemies like [[Gorr the God Butcher]] and the [[Shadow Monsters]]. ==Equipment== ===Weapons=== {{Quote|[[Mjølnir]] chose you. It chose you because you're worthy, and that's something. When I first met you, I was unworthy, I was unable to pick up the hammer. But you taught me there is no greater purpose than to help those in need. You made me worthy. So, whatever you want to do, we can do together. Well what do you want to do?"
"I'm gonna get those children back to their families. I'm gonna finish that mission.|[[Thor]] and Jane Foster|Thor: Love and Thunder}} *'''[[Mjølnir]]''': ''To be added'' ===Other Equipment=== *'''[[Phase Meter]]''': Foster used this device to measure the cosmic energy anomalies within the [[Nine Realms]]. This device was used when Foster came across a series of portals created due to the [[Convergence]]. {{Quote|This [[Magnetic-Propulsion Ball|thing]] is amazing! I mean the magnetic-propulsion alone would advance [[Earth]] science by decades.|Jane Foster|Thor: The Dark World}} *'''[[Magnetic-Propulsion Ball]]''': An [[Asgardian]] toy that Foster believed could be a scientific breakthrough for [[Earth]]. *'''[[Gravimetric Spike]]''': A device designed by [[Erik Selvig]] to stabilize the focal point of the [[Convergence]] and pass by its effects. Foster planted these devices to teleport the [[Dark Elves]] away, stalling their plans. *'''[[Winged Helmet]]''': ''To be added'' ===Former Equipment=== {{Quote|She stuck her hand inside a [[Chamber|rock]] this one time, and then the [[Aether]] stuck itself inside her.|[[Thor]] to the [[Avengers]]|Avengers: Endgame}} *'''[[Reality Stone]]''': [[File:Aether in Jane.png|thumb|250px|The [[Reality Stone]] flowing within Foster's body]] A strange red liquid, which was actually one of the [[Infinity Stones]], that absorbed itself into Foster's body, using her as a host to draw from and grow stronger. While fused with Foster, the Reality Stone would release a violent blast from her body to protect itself. It was finally extracted from her body by [[Malekith]], when she, [[Thor]] and [[Loki]] arrived at [[Svartalfheim]]. ===Vehicles=== {{Expand|TLaT}} *'''Campervan''': In preparation of a space anomaly, Foster, [[Darcy Lewis]], and [[Erik Selvig]] took a campervan out to [[New Mexico]]. There, they crashed into [[Thor]] and had to take him to the hospital. *'''[[Dark Elf Harrow]]''': Following [[Malekith]]'s [[Sacking of Asgard|attack]] on [[Asgard]], Foster joined [[Thor]] and [[Loki]] as they commandeered an abandoned Harrow left by the [[Dark Elves]]. As they escaped in the ship, Foster fainted and was carried off the ship to the [[Asgardian Skiff]]. *'''[[Asgardian Skiff]]''': After departing from the [[Dark Elf Harrow]], Foster boarded the ship with [[Thor]] and [[Loki]] as they escaped from [[Asgard]] to [[Svartalfheim]] and travelled to confront [[Malekith]]. *'''''[[Aegir]]''''': ''To be added'' ==Facilities== {{Expand|TLaT}} *'''[[Culver University]]''': Foster attended Culver University as a student and would eventually graduate. *'''[[Smith Motors]]''': [[File:Thor-disneyscreencaps com-4048.jpg|thumb|250px|Foster with [[Thor]] in her [[Smith Motors|Research Base]]]] When Foster moved to [[New Mexico]] to look into anomalies out in the desert surrounding [[Puente Antiguo]] with [[Erik Selvig]] and [[Darcy Lewis]], they operated out of a lab to conduct their research. When they met [[Thor]], they allowed him to stay at the base with them. The base was completely emptied out when [[S.H.I.E.L.D.]] arrived to confiscate their equipment, but later returned it so Foster could work from the base for S.H.I.E.L.D. *'''[[Jane Foster's Apartment]]''': [[File:Jane Apartment 5.png|thumb|250px|Foster reunites with her team in her [[Jane Foster's Apartment|apartment]]]] Originally her mother's place, Foster moved in to the apartment after she was called to [[London]] by [[Erik Selvig]]. Foster used the apartment as her lab, although she found herself spending all her time moping around, still hung up about [[Thor]]. When Foster returned to [[Earth]], she went back to her apartment where her team formulated a plan to stop [[Malekith]]. After the [[Convergence]], Foster witnessed Thor arrive at her apartment to live a life with her on Earth. *'''[[Royal Palace of Valaskjalf]]''': When [[Thor]] discovered something to be wrong with Foster, he brought her to [[Asgard]] to help her. After learning the that the [[Aether]] was inside her, Foster was allowed to stay on Asgard for safety, given her own chambers, which she was imprisoned in temporarily after the [[Sacking of Asgard]]. *'''[[University of London]]''': When living in [[London]], Foster found employment at the University of London, where she would continue her research in astrophysics, the [[Nine Realms]] and the [[Convergence]]. [[Thor]] visited Foster at the University, where she brought him to the observatory, telling him about her research. ==Relationships== {{Col-begin}}{{Col-2}} ===Family=== *Father † *[[Elaine Foster]] † - Mother ===Allies=== *[[Erik Selvig]] - Mentor and Close Friend *[[Darcy Lewis]] - Former Intern and Close Friend *[[Donald Blake]] - Ex-Boyfriend *[[Asgardians]] **[[Asgardian Royal Family]] ***[[Odin]] † ***[[Frigga]] † - Friend ***[[Thor]] - Ex-Boyfriend and Love Interest **[[Sif]] **[[Warriors Three]] ***[[Volstagg]] † ***[[Fandral]] † ***[[Hogun]] † **[[Heimdall]] † **[[Eir]] **[[Einherjar]] **[[Valkyrie|Brunnhilde/Valkyrie]] - Friend **[[Grace (Mother)|Grace]] **[[Axl Heimdallson]] *[[Isabela Alvarez]] - Waitress *[[S.H.I.E.L.D.]] **[[Phil Coulson]] † **[[Jasper Sitwell]] † *[[Richard Madison]] - Date *[[Ian Boothby]] *[[John (Child)|John]] *[[Maddie]] *[[Navid]] *[[Loki]] † - Temporary Enemy and Savior *[[Hulk|Bruce Banner/Hulk]] - Resurrector *[[Warsong]] *[[Korg]] *[[Miek]] *[[Toothgrinder]] *[[Toothgnasher]] *[[Council of Godheads]] **[[Bao]] **[[Ninny of the Nonny]] **[[Ennead]] ***[[Bast]] *[[Celestials]] *[[Cosmic Entities]] **[[Eternity]] *[[Gorr the God Butcher]] † - Former Enemy and Indirect Victim *[[Love]] {{Col-2}} ===Enemies=== *[[Destroyer]] † *[[Metropolitan Police Service]] *[[Dark Elves]] **[[Malekith]] † **[[Kurse|Algrim/Kurse]] † *[[Thanos]] † - Killer *[[Shadow Monsters]] *[[Olympians]] **[[Zeus]] *[[United States Department of Damage Control]] {{Col-end}} ==Appearances== {| class="mw-collapsible mw-collapsed wikitable" width="100%" ! Appearances for Mighty Thor |- | {{Scroll box|content= In chronological order: *{{Appearance|TLaT}} (flashbacks) *{{Appearance|T}} **{{Appearance|ThA1}} **{{Appearance|ThA2}} **{{Appearance|TAPFBW3}} (concurrent events) **{{Appearance|TAPFBW4}} (concurrent events) **{{Appearance|TTR}} **{{Appearance|TFAtE}} **{{Appearance|TATAA}} **{{Appearance|TTMARASaCD}} *{{Appearance|TTDWP1}} *{{Appearance|TA}} (photo & mentioned) **{{Appearance|TTDWP1}} (concurrent events) **{{Appearance|TTDWP2}} (concurrent events) *{{Appearance|TTDW}} **{{Appearance|TRP3}} **{{Appearance|TRP4}} **{{Appearance|TTDWRAS}} *{{Appearance|AoS115}} (mentioned) *{{Appearance|AOH}} *{{Appearance|AAoU}} (mentioned) *{{Appearance|WNF102}} (mentioned) *{{Appearance|TLaT}} (flashbacks) *{{Appearance|TR}} (mentioned) *{{Appearance|AE}} (photo & mentioned) *{{Appearance|TLaT}} *{{Appearance|SHAaL109}} (photo & mentioned) *{{Appearance|TLaT}} *{{Appearance|MM1}} (photo)[[:File:DODC Office 1.png|DODC Office Set Photo]] }} |} ==Trivia== *In the comics, Jane Foster was a nurse eventually took up the mantle of '''Thor''' after he lost his ability to wield [[Mjølnir]] and was stripped of his powers. **While Jane Foster became the new Thor of the original comics continuity in 2014's [[w:c:marvel:Thor Vol 4 1|''Thor'' #1]], an alternate universe version of her as Thor (under the name '''Thordis''') first debuted in a 1978 issue of ''[[w:c:marvel:What If? Vol 1 10|What If?]]'' *[[Natalie Portman]] desired the role of Foster because the character is an astrophysicist in the films despite being a registered nurse and medical doctor in the comics. ==Behind the Scenes== *[[Chris Hemsworth]] hosted an episode of ''{{WPS|Saturday Night Live}}'' on 7 March 2015 in which he portrayed [[Thor]] in an [[Avengers]]-themed skit. During the skit, Thor refers to Jane Foster as his "ex," leading some to speculate that they had broken up between the events of ''[[Thor: The Dark World]]'' and ''[[Avengers: Age of Ultron]]''. Chris Hemsworth later clarified that Thor and Jane were still together during that time in an interview.[https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=eRRfSseCe1g Avengers News Report - SNL] *[[Dorenda Moore]], [[Elsa Pataky]], [[Ashlee Fidow]], [[Zia Kelly]], [[Mandy Kowalski]] and [[Cecilia Johnson]] were stunt doubles for [[Natalie Portman]] in the role of Jane Foster. *[[Faye Sewell]] was a stand-in for [[Natalie Portman]] in the role of Jane Foster. *Portman expressed interest in having Foster appear in ''[[The Avengers]]'' but her pregnancy at the time meant that she couldn't appear in the film so her absence was explained as her working at an observatory in Norway alongside [[Darcy Lewis]]. *According to [[Natalie Portman]], the reason for which Jane Foster wasn't written into the story of ''[[Thor: Ragnarok]]'' was because of its setting: ''Obviously, I wasn't written into ''[[Thor: Ragnarok]]'' because of where it took place. It wasn't really on [[Earth]], and my character is on Earth''.[https://www.etonline.com/natalie-portman-talks-returning-to-marvel-for-thor-love-and-thunder-exclusive-133151 Natalie Portman Talks Returning to Marvel for 'Thor: Love and Thunder' (Exclusive)] *[[A.C. Bradley]] originally pitched an episode of ''[[What If...?]]'' featuring Jane Foster's transformation into Mighty Thor, but her idea was nixed because [[Taika Waititi]] was keeping it for ''[[Thor: Love and Thunder]]''.[https://discussingfilm.net/2019/09/06/5806/ ‘What If…?’ Showrunner Ashley Bradley Talks ‘Trollhunters’, The Beginning Of ‘What If…?’, Captain Carter, The Watcher & Star-Lord T’Challa (EXCLUSIVE)] *[[Natalie Portman]] revealed in an interview with ''{{WPS|Vanity Fair (magazine)|Vanity Fair}}'' that for Jane Foster's transformation into Mighty Thor in ''[[Thor: Love and Thunder]]'', she trained with [[Naomi Pendergast]] for four months prior to filming and throughout the entire filming process: ''"We did a lot of weight training and a lot of protein shakes—heavyweight training that I haven’t ever done before. Of course, I've never really aimed to get bulky. It was very physical, so it was a lot of both agility work and also strength work. It definitely helps you get into character, and it's definitely changed the way I move. You walk differently; you feel different. I mean, it's so wild to feel strong for the first time in my life."''[https://www.vanityfair.com/style/2021/08/natalie-portman-miss-dior-eau-de-parfum Natalie Portman on Summer Reading, Britney Spears, and the Scent of Love | Vanity Fair] *[[Anthony Winn]]'s storyboards for ''[[Thor: Love and Thunder]]'' show that early on during the film's production, upon becoming Mighty Thor, Jane Foster was intended to go on a superhero spree facing different enemies, [[Galactus]] included.[https://www.anthonywinnart.com/thorloveandthunder?pgid=l5tsqwyj-4de74053-10b4-4155-aa7c-34cdb1018b95 ''Thor: Love and Thunder'' - Anthony Lee Winn's Storyboards] *In ''[[w:c:marvel:Unbelievable Gwenpool Vol 1 2|Unbelievable Gwenpool Vol 1 #2]]'', When Gwenpool is in battle with Thor (Jane Foster), she tries to remember her name, at first only being able to remember that the MCU version was played by [[Natalie Portman]]. ==References== {{Gallery|Mighty Thor/Gallery}} {{Wq|Mighty Thor}} {{Reflist}} ==External Links== *{{Marvel|Jane Foster|Mighty Thor}} *{{WP|Jane Foster (comics)|Mighty Thor}} *{{WP|Jane Foster (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Mighty Thor}} *{{Marvel.com|characters/jane-foster}} [[de:Jane Foster]] [[es:Jane Foster]] [[fr:Jane Foster]] [[pl:Jane Foster]] [[pt-br:Jane Foster]] [[ru:Джейн Фостер]] [[Category:Characters]] [[Category:Thor (film) Characters]] [[Category:Thor: The Dark World Characters]] [[Category:Thor: Love and Thunder Characters]] [[Category:Comic Characters]] [[Category:Book Characters]] [[Category:Legends Characters]] [[Category:Humans]] [[Category:Females]] [[Category:Brown Hair]] [[Category:Brown Eyes]] [[Category:S.H.I.E.L.D.
